gloss.en: kept safe from harm or danger

words.example: This forest is a protected natural area.
They are protecting the village from floods.

gloss.en: substances in food that build the body

words.example: Eggs and beans are rich in proteins.
The test proved that he was right.
The school provides free books to students.
They are providing food for the workers.
Canada has ten provinces.

gloss.en: supplies of food, or rules in a law

words.example: They packed enough provisions for the trip.

gloss.en: books or papers that have been printed

words.example: Her research appeared in several publications.

gloss.en: printed and made available to read

words.example: The author published her first novel last year.

gloss.en: companies that produce books or papers

words.example: Several publishers wanted to print the book.

gloss.en: Spanish word for port, as in Puerto Rico

words.example: We flew to Puerto Rico for a holiday.

gloss.en: moved something toward you with force

words.example: She pulled the door open.
The doctor checked his pulmonary function.

gloss.en: school children; or dark centers of eyes

words.example: The teacher praised her pupils.
They purchased a new house last month.
The purchaser paid in cash.
She carried her purchases home in a bag.
He is purchasing a car this week.
This tool serves many purposes.

gloss.en: in agreement with a rule or law

words.example: He acted pursuant to the contract.
He pushed the heavy box across the floor.
She puts her keys on the table.
He is putting the books on the shelf.

gloss.en: skills or degrees that fit you for a job

words.example: She has the qualifications for the role.
Honesty is one of her best qualities.
The study used quantitative data.
They bought large quantities of rice.

gloss.en: relating to the physics of tiny particles

words.example: She studies quantum physics.
The company sends a quarterly report.

gloss.en: four equal parts; or living rooms

words.example: Cut the apple into quarters.
They moved to Quebec last summer.

gloss.en: things you ask to get an answer

words.example: The students asked many questions.
The article quoted the famous author.

gloss.en: distance from a circle's center to its edge

words.example: The circle has a radius of five centimeters.
Railroads connected the two cities.
The country built new railways.

gloss.en: lifted up, or brought up children

words.example: She raised her hand to ask a question.
They are raising money for the school.
Ralph is my older brother.
Prices cover a wide range of ranges.
Prices vary, ranging from low to high.
He rose through the ranks quickly.

gloss.en: speeds or amounts measured over time

words.example: Interest rates went up this year.
The recipe uses simple ratios of flour to water.
The old house had rats in the walls.
The sun's rays warmed the beach.

gloss.en: arrived at, or stretched out to

words.example: We reached the top of the hill.

gloss.en: arrives at, or stretches out to

words.example: The train reaches the city at noon.
She is reaching for the top shelf.
Their reactions to the news surprised me.

gloss.en: a machine that produces nuclear energy

words.example: The nuclear reactor powers the whole city.

gloss.en: people who read books or articles

words.example: The magazine has millions of readers.
The meter readings were higher today.

gloss.en: looks at and understands written words

words.example: She reads a book every week.
He realized he had left his keys at home.
She had good reasons for leaving early.

gloss.en: papers showing that money was paid

words.example: Keep your receipts for the refund.

gloss.en: got something that was given or sent

words.example: I received your letter yesterday.
She receives many emails each day.
He is receiving treatment at the hospital.

gloss.en: a cell part that responds to a signal

words.example: The drug blocks a receptor in the brain.

gloss.en: knew someone or something from before

words.example: I recognized her voice at once.

gloss.en: suggestions about what is best to do

words.example: The report made several recommendations.
The doctor recommended more rest.
They recorded the meeting on video.
The office keeps careful records.

gloss.en: got better, or got something back

words.example: She recovered quickly after the illness.

gloss.en: made smaller in size or amount

words.example: They reduced the price by half.

gloss.en: makes smaller in size or amount

words.example: Exercise reduces stress.

gloss.en: making smaller in size or amount

words.example: They are reducing waste in the office.
The paper lists all its references.

gloss.en: mentioned, or sent to another person

words.example: The doctor referred him to a specialist.
Are you referring to the new plan?
This word refers to a color.

gloss.en: showed back light, or thought deeply

words.example: The mirror reflected the bright light.
The lake reflects the mountains.
The government introduced new reforms.
She refused to sign the form.

gloss.en: thought of in a certain way

words.example: He is regarded as a great leader.
Please send my regards to your family.

gloss.en: large areas of a country or world

words.example: Different regions have different weather.

gloss.en: put a name on an official list

words.example: They registered for the course online.

gloss.en: a return to an earlier or worse state

words.example: The study used a regression model.
Banks are closely regulated by law.
The factory follows safety regulations.
They reinforced the bridge with steel.
The company rejected his offer.
This chapter relates to the last one.
We discussed issues relating to safety.
The two countries have good relations.
Strong relationships take time to build.
All my relatives came to the wedding.
The band released a new song.

gloss.en: stayed the same or in place

words.example: He remained calm during the storm.
Her remarks made everyone laugh.
She remembered his name at once.
He removed his shoes at the door.
They are removing the old paint.
The patient has a renal problem.
The shock rendered him speechless.

gloss.en: the act of making something new again

words.example: My passport is due for renewal.
The car needs some repairs.

gloss.en: put something new in place of another

words.example: They replaced the broken window.
She replied to my email quickly.
The news reported a big storm.

gloss.en: written or spoken accounts of facts

words.example: The manager read all the reports.

gloss.en: ways of showing or describing things

words.example: The map uses simple representations of roads.
The representatives met to discuss the plan.

gloss.en: acted for, or stood as a symbol of

words.example: She represented her school at the contest.

gloss.en: acting for, or standing as a symbol of

words.example: He is representing his country at the games.

gloss.en: acts for, or stands as a symbol of

words.example: This flag represents peace.
The popular book was reprinted twice.
The image was reproduced in the book.

gloss.en: relating to a republic or its party

words.example: He voted for the Republican candidate.
She requested a quiet room.
The office handles many requests each day.
The job required a lot of patience.

gloss.en: things that are needed or demanded

words.example: The course has strict requirements.
This recipe requires fresh eggs.
